title: Model, Surface, Capability, and Constraint Selection · Contract Clinic: Capability Discovery
summary: Choose between Claude and Codex surfaces for a repository and document task. Planned artifact: Add and verify the prompt capability discovery increment in a capability and surface decision matrix. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Verify current model and tool capabilities from official documentation rather than remembered names.
Choose chat, coding-agent, API, or workflow surfaces according to state, tools, latency, and review needs.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.prompt:
# Cross-agent prompt portfolio
# Add tested contracts below. Keep shared intent separate from Claude and Codex adaptations.

steps.title: Predict Capability Discovery
steps.instruction:
Use a changed theory case derived from this module: prompt model surface. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Use a changed theory case derived from this module: prompt model surface. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. Before reading, predict which action preserves the Capability Discovery contract.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for verify current model and tool capabilities from official documentation rather than remembered names.

content.text: Verify current model and tool capabilities from official documentation rather than remembered names. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Preserve authority, safety, accessibility, traceability, and stakeholder consequences alongside technical correctness.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: prompt model surface. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Capability Discovery, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a capability and surface decision matrix.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: A remembered model list remains current indefinitely. This fails the Capability Discovery contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

steps.title: Build Capability Discovery evidence
steps.instruction: Append a contract headed "Evidence: prompt-capability-discovery-1h5jjh" with concrete Goal, Context, Constraints, Output, and Done when sections tailored to this case.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Capability Discovery into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
Evidence: prompt-capability-discovery-1h5jjh
Goal: [replace with one observable outcome]
Context: [replace with decision-relevant facts]
Constraints: [replace with authority and safety boundaries]
Output: [replace with the artifact and evidence shape]
Done when: [replace with changed-case acceptance criteria]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

steps.title: Defend the Capability Discovery model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Capability Discovery applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Verify current model and tool capabilities from official documentation rather than remembered names.
